action.thought_summary: Starting the interview by clarifying the target users and what 'local' means, as this shapes the entire app scope and interaction design.
action.question: What does 'local' mean in your context—same city, same neighborhood, or within a certain radius? And who are the primary users: any job seeker, or a specific group like students, tradespeople, or career changers?

action.thought_summary: User mentioned Google Maps integration and location proximity is important. They invited questions about how location filtering should work. This falls under interaction behavior, which I should probe by turn 3. I'll ask a concrete question about how users should interact with location-based job filtering.
action.question: How should users interact with the location filtering—should they set a specific radius (e.g., 5, 10, 25 miles) from their current position, or should they be able to pan and zoom the map and see jobs within the visible area?

action.thought_summary: User has three core features: job finding with maps, learning resources, and communication. Location filtering details are uncertain. Moving to probe the communication feature, which is another interaction element, to understand what type of messaging and with whom.
action.question: For the communication feature, who should users be able to communicate with—employers about job listings, other job seekers for networking, or both? And what type of communication: direct messaging, forums, or group chats?

action.thought_summary: User has been uncertain about interaction details. Need to probe more concretely. Haven't explored content/data needs for job listings yet - what information should each job posting display? This is a core feature that needs definition.
action.question: When a user views a job listing, what key information should be displayed—such as job title, company name, salary range, required qualifications, application deadline, or job type (full-time/part-time/contract)?

action.thought_summary: User has been uncertain on many specifics but mentioned LMS for career-relevant skills as a concrete feature. Now at turn 5, should focus on content/data needs. Probing the learning resources feature since it's a distinct content area the user explicitly mentioned.
action.question: You mentioned a learning management system for career-relevant skills—should the learning content be linked to specific job listings (e.g., 'complete this course to qualify for this job'), or should it be a standalone resource library that users browse independently?

action.thought_summary: User has been consistently uncertain about specifics. After 5 turns covering interaction and content, I should try a scenario-based question about a core workflow—job application—to help them articulate concrete needs. This is still interaction behavior but from a different angle.
action.question: When a user wants to apply for a job they found on the map, should they be redirected to an external website or employer portal, or should they be able to submit an application directly within the app with a saved resume and profile?

action.thought_summary: The user has been vague on most specifics but mentioned social profile connection and secure login. Style/UI category is overdue per coverage schedule. I'll ask about visual/UI preferences for the app to cover that category.
action.question: You mentioned connecting social profiles—should users be able to log in using social accounts (like Google or LinkedIn), and should their profile information from those accounts auto-fill their job seeker profile in the app?

action.thought_summary: The user has been consistently uncertain about specifics. Style/UI category is overdue per coverage schedule. I should ask a concrete question about visual/layout preferences to elicit implicit requirements in that area.
action.question: For the app's home screen, should the map view be the primary focus with job listings appearing as a secondary panel, or should there be a traditional list view with an option to open the map when needed?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many details but confirmed social login and map integration. Job search filtering beyond location (keywords, salary, job type) hasn't been explored yet, which is a core content/data need for a job search app.
action.question: Beyond location, should users be able to filter job search results by criteria like keywords, salary range, job type (full-time/part-time/contract), or experience level—and if so, which filters are most important to include?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain on many specifics but has confirmed social login, map integration, and LMS access. Notifications/alerts are a critical interaction feature for job search apps that hasn't been explored yet—users need to know when new jobs match their criteria or when they receive messages.
action.question: Should the app send push notifications or alerts to users—for example, when new jobs matching their criteria are posted, when they receive a message from an employer, or when a learning course they're enrolled in has updates?

action.thought_summary: User confirmed notifications are important but hasn't specified control granularity. This is a concrete follow-up to the last exchange about notifications, which falls under interaction behavior. After this, should move to style/UI category which hasn't been covered yet.
action.question: Should users be able to customize which notification types they receive—for example, turning off course updates while keeping job alerts and employer messages—or should all notifications be on by default with no individual toggles?

action.thought_summary: The user has been largely undecided on many specifics but has confirmed Google Maps integration, social login, and notifications. The middleware warning suggests covering style/UI preferences, which hasn't been adequately explored yet. I should ask a focused question about visual style or UI theme.
action.question: What visual style or theme do you envision for the app—for example, a clean professional look with neutral colors suited for career-focused users, or a more vibrant modern design with bold accents? Do you have any brand colors or design references in mind?

action.thought_summary: User specified light goldenrod yellow background and olive drab elements. Need to probe how these colors apply to specific UI components and whether there are other style considerations like typography or accessibility.
action.question: How should the olive drab color be used across key interactive elements—for example, should primary action buttons, navigation bar, and job listing cards all use olive drab, or should different shades be used to create visual hierarchy between clickable actions and static elements?

action.thought_summary: The user has specified two colors (light goldenrod yellow background, olive drab elements) but hasn't defined other visual aspects. The middleware suggests focusing on style/UI. Typography and icon style are important visual elements that affect readability and brand identity, especially for a career-focused app.
action.question: What typography style do you prefer for the app—should it use a clean sans-serif font for a modern readable feel, or a serif font for a more traditional professional tone? And should icons be minimalist line-style or filled solid-style to match the olive drab elements?

action.thought_summary: The user has specified colors but remains undecided on typography and icons. Rather than pressing further on those, I should explore another UI/layout aspect like navigation structure, which affects both interaction and style and hasn't been addressed yet.
action.question: How should the app's main navigation be structured—should it use a bottom tab bar with sections like Home, Map, Learning, Messages, and Profile, or a side drawer menu, or a top navigation bar? And which sections do you consider essential to always be accessible from the main navigation?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain on many specifics but has confirmed some concrete preferences: light goldenrod yellow background, olive drab elements, Google Maps integration, social login, notifications, and LMS access. With 5 turns remaining and the middleware suggesting UI focus, I should ask about a concrete UI aspect that could reveal implicit requirements—specifically how job markers/cards appear on the map versus in a list, which ties together content display and visual design.
action.question: When a job marker is tapped on the map, should a compact preview card slide up showing just the essentials (job title, company, distance), with a 'View Details' option to see the full listing—or should the full job details page open immediately from the map tap?

action.thought_summary: The user has specified light goldenrod yellow background and olive drab elements but hasn't addressed dark mode or alternative themes. This is a concrete UI/style question that could reveal whether the app needs theme flexibility or a single fixed theme.
action.question: Should the app support a dark mode option that users can toggle, or will it only use the light goldenrod yellow and olive drab theme you described?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many details but has established key preferences: light goldenrod yellow background, olive drab elements, Google Maps integration, LMS access, social login, and notifications. The middleware suggests focusing on visual style/UI. A concrete UI question about job listing card design would be useful since it's a core component affecting both map and list views.
action.question: For job listing cards in the list view, should they display a compact single-line layout showing just job title and company to maximize visible results, or a multi-line card with more details like salary, job type, and a small company logo thumbnail for quicker scanning without opening each listing?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many specifics, but core features are established: Google Maps integration, LMS access, social login, notifications, and specific color preferences. Communication details and the job application workflow remain undefined. I should probe the application workflow since it's a critical interaction connecting job discovery to action.
action.question: When a user wants to save a job for later without applying immediately, should the app offer a 'bookmark' or 'save job' feature—and if so, should saved jobs be accessible from a dedicated section in the user's profile, or integrated into the map view as a separate layer?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many specifics but has revealed key preferences: Google Maps integration, LMS access, social login, notifications, and specific color scheme (light goldenrod yellow + olive drab). At turn 18/20, I should probe one more area that could reveal implicit requirements before finishing. The user profile and its role in job matching/recommendations hasn't been explored - this could uncover personalization requirements.
action.question: Should the app recommend jobs to users based on their profile information and skills—such as suggesting 'jobs you may be interested in' on the home screen—or should users always initiate searches manually without any algorithmic suggestions?
